steps.title: Spot the noun the opening phrase characterizes
steps.content: The phrase “Far from being modern inventions,” is an introductory adverbial phrase that must be followed by an independent clause. The noun being characterized by this opening phrase—drinking straws (implied)—must appear as the grammatical subject of the main clause.

steps.title: Select the clause with the correct subject
steps.content:
Choice B places “drinking straws” directly as the grammatical subject (“drinking straws were used by Sumerians in ancient Mesopotamia”), positioning the characterized noun where the opening phrase applies to it.
The other choices either place Sumerians or Mesopotamia as the subject (A and D) or bury drinking straws in a noun phrase (C), all of which misalign the opening characterization from the main clause's focus.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
